title: Dubautia molokaiensis
intro: Dubautia molokaiensis, also known as Molokai Dubautia, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Asteraceae family, native to the island of Molokai in Hawaii. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dubautia molokaiensis include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbalism, Dubautia molokaiensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in salves and tinctures, to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Dubautia molokaiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Dubautia and is native to the island of Molokai in Hawaii. The plant is a member of the tribe Madieae and is closely related to other species within the same genus. Further classification includes its position within the subfamily Asteroideae and the order Asterales. This classification helps to identify the plant's evolutionary relationships and position within the plant kingdom.
